column5: Q-word (誰) always takes が as subject marker; は marks known topic, not Q-words
column6: Rule: question-word subjects always use が
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:は frequency:top10k complexity:intro source:curated-jpgram

column5: がいます/あります introduces the existence of something new; は sets a known topic
column6: Existence sentences (います/あります) use が to introduce
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:は frequency:top1k complexity:intro source:curated-jpgram

column5: は marks (I) as the topic of self-introduction; the comment follows
column6: Self-introductions use は to set the topic about oneself
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:が frequency:top1k complexity:advanced source:curated-jpgram

column5: 読む is an action verb → で marks where actions take place; に marks static location
column6: Action verbs use で; verbs of existence (いる/ある) use に
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:に frequency:top10k complexity:standard source:curated-jpgram

column5: あります = existence verb → に marks where the thing exists
column6: Existence (ある/いる) always takes に for location
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:で frequency:top1k complexity:standard source:curated-jpgram

column5: Both work but に emphasises arriving at the specific destination; へ emphasises direction
column6: に = destination (arrival focus); へ = direction (movement focus, more literary)
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core frequency:top10k complexity:advanced confusable-with:へ source:curated-jpgram

column1: 疲れた___、休みます。(objective polite reason)
column5: ので gives objective/polite reason, preferred in formal speech; から is more direct/personal
column6: (this form) = polite objective cause; から = personal/direct cause
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:ので frequency:top10k complexity:advanced source:curated-jpgram confusable-with:から

column5: 質問する targets a person with に; を marks a direct object (but 質問する takes に)
column6: 質問する、相談する, etc. take に for the person addressed
column8: module:00-foundation jlpt:n5 source:shin-kanzen point:particles-core confusable-with:を frequency:top1k complexity:intro source:curated-jpgram
